mnemonics.title: ARQ Protocol Types
mnemonics.content: SGS: Stop-and-Wait, Go-Back-N, Selective Repeat. Remember 'Super Good Systems' use all three ARQ methods

mnemonics.title: Error Handling Steps
mnemonics.content: DARN: Detect error → Acknowledge status → Retransmit if needed → Notify completion. Like darning a sock (fixing holes)

mnemonics.title: Frame Sequence
mnemonics.content: NSACK: Next Sequence Number, Acknowledgement. Remember 'Never Start A Conversation Cold' for frame sequencing

mnemonics.title: Sliding Window Sizes
mnemonics.content: 1-2-3 Rule: Stop-and-Wait (window=1), Go-Back-N (sender=N, receiver=1), Selective Repeat (both=N)

acronyms.usage: Core protocol family for error control (Stop-and-Wait, Go-Back-N, Selective Repeat)
Receiver's way of saying 'Not Acknowledged - resend!'
Error detection using polynomial division (Critical for checksum calculations)
Proactive error handling using redundancy bits

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two overlapping photo frames (sender/receiver windows) sliding along a film strip of frames
Picture a kitchen scale - data bits are ingredients, checksum is the total weight to verify recipe
Visualize a train (N cars) - if car 3 derails, engine must return to car 3 and bring all subsequent cars again
Like a mailman only redelivering lost parcels (error frames) instead of all packages

analogies.analogy: Like video call participants: 'Did you get that? Yes. Next point...' after every sentence
Book page numbers - helps ensure no pages are missing and they're in order
Spellcheck (detection) vs Auto-correct (correction) in word processors
Highway system: Flow control = traffic lights, Error control = tow trucks/repair crews
